result:
Done. Roles and permissions are wired in and verified end to end against the running app.

**What was added**

- **`@fonderie/permissions` registered** (`src/fonderie.ts`) with `superRole: 'ADMIN'`, plus its migration SQL vendored to `migrations/permissions/` (the published tarball omits it, per the known gotcha).
- **`src/workspace-guard.ts`** — an app-level middleware enforcing permission checks on every workspace-scoped route, including the ones `@fonderie/workspaces` registers itself. It maps each route to a permission key (`workspaces.workspace|roles|members|invitations|settings`) and a CRUD operation (with overrides, e.g. `POST /workspaces/archive` = delete), resolves the session, and calls `PermissionsEngine.assert`. Non-members hold no roles, so they're denied automatically; missing `x-workspace-id` is a 400, no token a 401, denial a JSON 403.

**What came free from the bricks** — the workspaces module already ships ADMIN/GUEST system roles (seeded in its migration), multi-role membership (one row per user/workspace/role), and all requested endpoints: `POST /workspaces/roles` (custom role), `POST /workspaces/members/:userId/roles` (assign), `DELETE /workspaces/members/:userId/roles/:roleId` (remove), `POST /workspaces/roles/:roleId/permissions` (set permissions).

**Design decisions worth knowing**

- **ADMIN** is enforced via the engine's `superRole` bypass; **GUEST** is read-only via a code fallback in the guard. That's deliberate: the permissions table has `UNIQUE(role_id, permission_key)` and the engine only matches rows scoped to the checked workspace, so a shared system role physically can't carry per-workspace permission rows — both system roles' semantics have to live in code. Custom roles (e.g. EDITOR) use normal per-workspace permission rows set through the API.
- Two integration surprises the docs don't mention (now saved to memory): `requirePermission`/`requireRole` can't run in an app-level `.use()` (their engine meta isn't populated yet — the guard calls `engine.assert` directly), and stored wildcard keys (`'*'`) never match.

**Verified live** (server booted against local Postgres): guest denied role-create/member-delete (403), guest allowed reads (200), guest's EDITOR grant allowed settings update (200) and was revoked immediately after role removal (403), admin full access via super role, unauthenticated 401, foreign-workspace access 403. Typecheck clean; test server stopped and temp test roles cleaned up.
